3.why: Union-find needs one slot per account.
3.invariant: n is fixed for the whole run.

4.why: parent[i] is the account i currently points to in its set.
4.invariant: Following parents always leads to a root.

5.why: Each starts in its own one-account set.
5.invariant: Every account gets a parent slot.

6.happening: Account {i} starts as its own parent.
6.why: Before any merge, each account is its own set and its own root.
6.invariant: parent[i] == i means i is a root.

7.happening: Start the list of graph nodes.
7.why: Each account is drawn as a node.

9.why: The name is shown; note that same-name accounts are still separate nodes.
9.invariant: Node positions stay fixed for the whole run.

10.why: A link is drawn each time two sets are unioned.

11.why: The first account to claim an email owns it; a later match triggers a union.
11.invariant: email_owner[e] is the account that first saw email e.

12.why: We process accounts one at a time.
12.invariant: i is the account being scanned.

13.why: Every account's emails must be examined.

13.happening: All accounts scanned — the forest is fully built.
13.why: Every shared email has produced a union.
13.invariant: Connected accounts now share a root.

14.why: It holds the name then the emails.
14.invariant: acc[0] is the name, acc[1:] the emails.

15.why: Index 0 is the name, so emails begin at 1.
15.invariant: j indexes into this account's emails.

16.why: Each email may link this account to another.

16.happening: This account's emails are all processed.
16.why: Move on to the next account.
16.invariant: Every email of account {i} has been claimed or matched.

17.why: We check whether another account already owns it.
17.invariant: email is one of account {i}'s addresses.

18.happening: '{email}' is already owned by account {email_owner[email]} — these accounts must be the same person.
18.why: A shared email is the signal to union two accounts.
18.invariant: A shared email always merges two sets.

18.happening: '{email}' is new — no one owns it yet.
18.invariant: Only the first account to see an email claims it.

19.why: We will union this account with that one.
19.invariant: other is the account that first claimed the email.

20.happening: Start walking account {i} up to its root.
20.why: Union works on set ROOTS, not arbitrary members.
20.invariant: rx will end at account {i}'s root.

21.happening: Account {rx} isn't a root — climb to its parent.
21.why: The root is the one node that points to itself.
21.invariant: Each step moves closer to the root.

21.happening: Account {rx} is a root (points to itself).
21.why: The walk stops at the set's representative.

22.why: This account was already merged, so its root is higher up.

23.happening: Start walking account {other} up to its root.
23.why: We need the other set's root too.
23.invariant: ry will end at account {other}'s root.

24.happening: Account {ry} isn't a root — climb to its parent.
24.why: We follow parents until the self-pointing root.
24.invariant: Each step moves closer to the root.

24.why: The walk stops at the other set's representative.

25.why: This account was already merged, so its root is higher up.

26.happening: Roots {rx} and {ry} differ — merge the two sets.
26.why: Different roots mean the accounts are in different sets that must join.

26.happening: Roots {rx} and {ry} are the same — already merged.
26.why: The accounts are already in one set, so nothing to do.
26.invariant: A set is never merged with itself.

27.happening: Point root {rx} at root {ry} — the union.
27.why: Attaching one root under the other fuses the two sets into one.
27.invariant: Account {rx} is no longer a root.

28.why: The new parent pointer is now part of the forest.
28.invariant: links always mirrors the parent array.

30.why: It is the first account to see this email.

33.happening: Create the map from root to its emails.
33.why: Now that the forest is built, gather each set's emails.
33.invariant: groups[r] lists the emails of the person rooted at r.

34.why: Each email belongs to exactly one merged person.
34.invariant: Every claimed email is grouped once.

35.why: We follow that account to its root to find its group.
35.invariant: owner is where this email entered the forest.

36.happening: Start walking account {owner} up to its root.
36.why: The root identifies which merged person the email belongs to.
36.invariant: root will end at the set's representative.

37.happening: Account {root} isn't a root — climb to its parent.
37.why: The email's group is keyed by the set's root.
37.invariant: Each step moves closer to the root.

37.why: This root is the group key for the email.
37.invariant: root is the representative of owner's set.

38.why: Merged accounts sit below their root.

39.happening: Root {root} already has a group — add '{email}' to it.
39.why: All emails under the same root belong to one person.

39.happening: Root {root} has no group yet — start one.
39.why: This is the first email seen for that person.

40.why: It belongs to the same merged person.
40.invariant: The group collects all of the person's emails.

42.why: First email seen for this merged person.
42.invariant: The group collects all of the person's emails.

43.happening: Prepare to collect the group roots.
43.why: We sort them so the output order is deterministic.
43.invariant: roots will list every group's representative.

44.why: Each root becomes one merged account in the output.

45.why: We collect the roots before sorting them.

46.why: A fixed order makes the simulation reproducible.
46.invariant: Output groups appear in a stable order.

48.happening: Start at the first sorted root.
48.why: Build one merged account per group, in order.

49.why: Each group needs its name and sorted emails assembled.

49.invariant: result holds one row per merged person.

50.invariant: root is the current group's representative.

51.why: They will be sorted and listed after the name.
51.invariant: emails holds every address of this person.

52.why: The problem requires the emails in sorted order.
52.invariant: emails is now in ascending order.

53.why: Every account in the set shares this name.
53.invariant: The name is taken from the root account's row.

54.happening: Start the merged account with the name.
54.why: The output format is [name, then sorted emails].

55.happening: Start at the first sorted email.
55.why: Append each email after the name.

56.why: The merged account lists them all after the name.
56.invariant: Emails are added in sorted order.

57.why: It is one of this person's addresses.

58.why: Add every email of the group.

59.happening: Add the finished merged account to the result.

61.why: Every set of accounts sharing an email has become one person.

init.happening: Drawing one node per account and setting up union-find.
init.why: Each account starts as its own set.

loop.happening: Scanning accounts and emails, or assembling output groups.
loop.why: Shared emails drive the merges; roots drive the grouping.

check.happening: Testing email ownership, root difference, or group existence.
check.why: These branches decide when to union and how to group.
check.invariant: No state changes on a pure check line.

update.happening: Claiming an email, performing a union, or collecting a group.
update.why: These lines change the forest or build the answer.
update.invariant: A union always draws a link and re-roots one tree.

move.happening: Walking parent pointers up to a root, or advancing a cursor.
move.why: Find follows parents until it reaches the self-pointing root.
move.invariant: Following parents always terminates at a root.

return.why: All shared-email accounts have been fused.
